meta.title: chiDEV Middleware Prior Art and Branch Review
meta.description: Evidence-first review of middleware prior art, current middleware reality, chiDEV branch file-by-file calls, and graph/prior-art direction.

props.title: chiDEV Middleware Prior Art and Branch Review
props.subtitle: What the middleware idea was, what exists now, what pr86 and pr87 still add, and where retained ideas should live.

props.content: The core answer is: yes, middleware exists in current main, but the repo canon explicitly treats it as a thin transitional runtime wrapper. FlowMind, hooks, LevEvent, and orchestration remain the real ownership surfaces. The right question for the old chiDEV branches is therefore not whether to merge middleware, but which ideas or examples are still worth preserving as references.

props.label: CURRENT STATUS
props.title: Where the repo stands right now

props.label: PROVENANCE
props.title: What the prior art says about middleware vs hooks / FlowMind

props.content: The repo contains both the experiment and the correction. The DeerFlow branch work introduced a middleware chain model, but the canonical docs later constrain that surface and explicitly move declarative policy back to FlowMind and orchestration. There is also a later freeze note that says Poly owns the WHAT and FlowMind owns the WHEN.

rows.finding: Harness middleware is a transitional runtime decorator surface, not the canonical owner of context policy.
rows.why: This is the clearest current canon statement.

rows.finding: FlowMind owns context composition, lifecycle routing, loop policy, and guard semantics. Harness owns transport/runtime glue.
rows.why: This is the broader owner map, not just the middleware-specific one.

rows.finding: FlowMind owns topology, iteration, gates, and sequencing. Poly owns invoke/task/receipt semantics.
rows.why: This freezes the architectural split after the branch experiments.

rows.finding: Hooks are documented mainly through the archived agent-adapter path.
rows.why: Useful lineage, but not the current runtime owner.

rows.finding: DeerFlow middleware chain is treated as pattern source, not ownership template.
rows.why: This is where the experiment entered the repo.

props.title: Plain-English answer
props.content: You were remembering the **decision** correctly. The repo still has middleware code, but the repo no longer treats middleware as the place where policy truth should live.

Yes. Main has a real middleware chain and concrete modules under core/harness. What matters is how we classify them. The repo already classifies some as enabled runtime-only helpers and others as disabled policy-ish experiments.

rows.call: Exists, but the config says FlowMind should own dynamic context composition.
Exists, but policy ownership is intentionally not canonical here.
Exists as an opt-in long-session helper, not the final compaction architecture.
This is the kind of middleware the canon still allows: transport/runtime fence.

props.content: This branch is worth keeping around only as a mining/reference branch. The key distinction for each file is: what idea the branch was trying to add, what current main already has, and where we should preserve the idea if we keep it.

rows.branchIdea: Introduce a daemon lifecycle facade over adapters and dependency ordering.
rows.currentMain: Main has daemon runtime pieces but not this exact facade file.
rows.store: Reference implementation note or fresh extraction branch, not direct cherry-pick.

rows.branchIdea: Strict root schema plus permissive fallback variant.
rows.currentMain: Main still has the looser passthrough global schema.
rows.store: Config-schema design follow-up; compare against current config canon before coding.

rows.branchIdea: Tighten sentinel structure and lane shape.
rows.currentMain: Main already has the sentinel workflow and newer action pins.
rows.store: Sentinel tuning notes, not a branch merge.

rows.branchIdea: Alter expected-failure regexes and failure classification.
rows.currentMain: Main has the active expected-failure list.
Main already has the real sentinel test.
Main has a newer CLI surface.
Main has a live execution type system that moved further.
rows.store: Execution-shape notes if we reopen harness API cleanup.

rows.currentMain: Main already has these modules and disables the policy-ish ones.
rows.store: Current main + middleware specs already preserve the idea.

rows.currentMain: Main has its own scheduling surfaces.

props.content: This branch still has unmatched patches, but they should still be treated as reference ideas. The question is whether the branch contains useful examples for future FlowMind-first design, not whether it should shape current runtime ownership.

rows.branchIdea: Harness-owned loop(), graph(), graphEpic() convenience and alternate execution sequencing.
rows.currentMain: Main keeps harness execution narrower and does not expose that branch shape.
rows.store: Harness/orchestration design note, not code merge.

rows.branchIdea: DeerFlow-inspired middleware chain and extensibility wiring.
rows.currentMain: Main already has a middleware chain plus a spec that constrains it.
rows.store: Current main + spec-harness-middleware.md preserve the experiment.

rows.currentMain: Main already has a live harness.middleware chain declaration.
Main still has the example, but the branch adds a small semantic tweak.

rows.branchIdea: Document the DeerFlow harness feature extraction as pattern intake.
rows.currentMain: This doc already exists in main.

rows.branchIdea: Formalize middleware as transitional and constrained.
rows.currentMain: Already exists and is the current canon.

rows.branchIdea: Broader harness ownership framing around runtime decorators and lifecycle bridge.
rows.currentMain: Current main already has the evolved version.

rows.branchIdea: Fix cyclic deps and control-plane glue around harness.

props.title: chiDEV
props.content: This is the branch set we just triaged and cleaned. The meaningful leftovers are now only `pr86` and `pr87`, both as reference-idea branches, not merge candidates.

props.title: chiTOWN
props.content: This is your partner contributor’s work. Based on your note, his meaningful direction is in open PRs around product direction and Bayesian graph work. That means the next pass should be PR-first, not branch-first.

props.label: GRAPH DIRECTION
props.title: What the branch/worktree warm-cache and graph direction already has

props.content: There is real repo prior art for a graph-backed or graph-plus-vector direction. The strongest pattern is not “put all markdown into one giant vector store and stop.” The stronger direction is graph-backed primary structure with vectorized markdown summaries and/or chunks as secondary retrieval aids.

rows.implication: The repo already leans toward hybrid retrieval, not vector-only.
Harvester already emits graph-facing artifacts that could back prior-art lookup.

rows.evidence: Multiple workshop analyses and graph-storage adapter notes
rows.implication: Markdown should likely stay filesystem-backed, with graph nodes carrying summaries, refs, hashes, and optionally embeddings/chunk projections.

rows.evidence: Current repo has many worktrees and branch-local archaeology pain; no clean branch cache product surface exists yet.
rows.implication: A warm cache of branch/worktree graph projections would make prior-art queries much cheaper than brute-force grep across every ref.

props.label: FEEDBACK
props.title: Decision Review — per item
props.subtitle: Each item has 3 concrete choices, a recommended option with rationale, an Other field, and a notes box. Auto-saved locally; copy/export controls included.

const root=el.querySelector('#review-groups'); const status=el.querySelector('#review-status'); const counts=el.querySelector('#review-counts'); const stateLoad=()=>{try{return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY)||'{}')}catch{return {}}}; let state=stateLoad(); const persist=()=>{localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(state)); const decided=items.filter(item=>state[item.id]?.choice || state[item.id]?.other).length; counts.textContent=decided+' / '+items.length+' items have a decision'; status.textContent='Saved locally at '+new Date().toLocaleTimeString();}; const escapeHtml=(s)=>String(s||'').replace(/[&<>"]/g,(c)=>({'&':'&','<':'<','>':'>','"':'"'}[c])); const grouped=items.reduce((acc,item)=>{(acc[item.group]||(acc[item.group]=[])).push(item); return acc;},{}); root.innerHTML=''; Object.entries(grouped).forEach(([group,list])=>{ const section=document.createElement('section'); section.className='review-group'; const title=document.createElement('div'); title.className='review-group__title'; title.textContent=group; section.appendChild(title); const grid=document.createElement('div'); grid.className='review-grid'; list.forEach((item)=>{ const saved=state[item.id]||{}; const card=document.createElement('article'); card.className='review-card'; card.innerHTML='<div class="review-card__head"><div class="review-card__meta"><div><div class="review-card__group">'+escapeHtml(item.group)+'</div><div class="review-card__title">'+escapeHtml(item.title)+'</div></div></div><div class="review-card__summary"><strong class="review-mini">Decision context</strong>'+escapeHtml(item.context)+'</div><div class="review-card__current"><strong class="review-mini">Current state</strong>'+escapeHtml(item.current)+'</div><div class="review-callout"><strong>Recommended</strong><p>'+escapeHtml(item.options.find(o=>o[0]===item.recommended)?.[1]||item.recommended)+' — '+escapeHtml(item.why)+'</p></div><div class="review-card__store"><strong class="review-mini">If kept, store it as</strong>'+escapeHtml(item.store)+'</div></div>'; const options=document.createElement('div'); options.className='review-options'; item.options.forEach((opt,idx)=>{ const wrap=document.createElement('label'); wrap.className='review-option'; wrap.dataset.recommended=String(opt[0]===item.recommended); wrap.innerHTML='<input type="radio" name="choice-'+item.id+'" value="'+escapeHtml(opt[0])+'" '+(saved.choice===opt[0]?'checked':'')+'><div><div class="review-option__label">'+String.fromCharCode(97+idx)+'. '+escapeHtml(opt[1])+(opt[0]===item.recommended?' (Recommended)':'')+'</div><div class="review-option__desc">'+escapeHtml(opt[2])+'</div></div>'; options.appendChild(wrap); }); card.appendChild(options); const other=document.createElement('div'); other.className='review-other'; other.innerHTML='<div class="review-mini">Other</div><input type="text" placeholder="Other option or nuance" value="'+escapeHtml(saved.other||'')+'">'; card.appendChild(other); const notes=document.createElement('div'); notes.className='review-notes'; notes.innerHTML='<div class="review-mini">Notes</div><textarea placeholder="Reasoning, caveats, constraints, or rebuttal">'+escapeHtml(saved.notes||'')+'</textarea>'; card.appendChild(notes); card.querySelectorAll('input[type=radio]').forEach(r=>r.addEventListener('change',()=>{state[item.id]={...(state[item.id]||{}),choice:r.value}; persist();})); other.querySelector('input').addEventListener('input',(e)=>{state[item.id]={...(state[item.id]||{}),other:e.target.value}; persist();}); notes.querySelector('textarea').addEventListener('input',(e)=>{state[item.id]={...(state[item.id]||{}),notes:e.target.value}; persist();}); grid.appendChild(card); }); section.appendChild(grid); root.appendChild(section); }); const copyBtn=el.querySelector('#copy-json'); copyBtn.addEventListener('click',async()=>{ const payload={page:'chidev-middleware-review-v3',savedAt:new Date().toISOString(),state}; const text=JSON.stringify(payload,null,2); try{ await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text); status.textContent='Copied review JSON to clipboard'; }catch{ status.textContent='Clipboard copy failed'; } }); const dlBtn=el.querySelector('#download-json'); dlBtn.addEventListener('click',()=>{ const payload={page:'chidev-middleware-review-v3',savedAt:new Date().toISOString(),state}; const blob=new Blob([JSON.stringify(payload,null,2)],{type:'application/json'}); const url=URL.createObjectURL(blob); const a=document.createElement('a'); a.href=url; a.download='chidev-middleware-review.json'; a.click(); URL.revokeObjectURL(url); status.textContent='Downloaded review JSON'; }); el.querySelector('#reset-review').addEventListener('click',()=>{ localStorage.removeItem(STORAGE_KEY); state={}; status.textContent='Reset local review state'; counts.textContent='0 / '+items.length+' items have a decision'; root.querySelectorAll('input[type=radio]').forEach(i=>i.checked=false); root.querySelectorAll('.review-other input').forEach(i=>i.value=''); root.querySelectorAll('.review-notes textarea').forEach(i=>i.value=''); }); const decided=items.filter(item=>state[item.id]?.choice || state[item.id]?.other).length; counts.textContent=decided+' / '+items.length+' items have a decision'; status.textContent=decided ? 'Loaded saved local feedback' : 'No saved feedback yet';
